mission.title: Install and Configure Trivy Operator (Aqua Security)
mission.description: The Trivy Operator is a Kubernetes-native security toolkit that continuously scans your cluster for vulnerabilities and compliance issues. It automates security reporting through Kubernetes Custom Resource Definitions, making it easy to monitor security risks in real-time. Key trade-offs include the need for proper RBAC configurations and potential performance impacts during scans.

steps.title: Add the Trivy Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Trivy Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add aquasecurity https://aquasecurity.github.io/helm-charts/

```

> This allows you to install the Trivy Operator using Helm.

steps.title: Install the Trivy Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Trivy Operator in the 'trivy-system' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

kubectl create namespace trivy-system

helm install trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.32.1

```

> Create a dedicated namespace for the Trivy Operator.
> Install the Trivy Operator Helm chart.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Trivy Operator pods are running correctly in the specified namespace.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> You should see the Trivy Operator pods in a Running state.

steps.title: Create a sample Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates a sample Custom Resource to initiate a vulnerability scan.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: sample-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Pod
    name: sample-pod
    namespace: default
EOF

```

> This creates a sample VulnerabilityReport resource.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1
kind: VulnerabilityReport
metadata:
  name: sample-vulnerability-report
  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Pod
    name: sample-pod
    namespace: default
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource
steps.description:
Check if the Custom Resource created is ready and has generated a report.

```bash

kubectl get vulnerabilityreport -n default

```

> You should see the status of the VulnerabilityReport.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Trivy Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Trivy Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all related Custom Resource Definitions and reports, leading to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system

kubectl delete namespace trivy-system

```

> Uninstalls the Trivy Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Trivy Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to back up any important reports. This step will upgrade the Trivy Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get vulnerabilityreport -n default -o yaml > backup-vulnerability-reports.yaml

helm upgrade trivy-operator aquasecurity/trivy-operator --namespace trivy-system --version 0.32.1

kubectl get pods -n trivy-system

```

> Backup existing VulnerabilityReports.
> Upgrade the Trivy Operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.
commands: kubectl get vulnerabilityreport -n default -o yaml > backup-vulnerability-reports.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "VulnerabilityReport" in version "trivy.aquasecurity.github.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definition for VulnerabilityReport may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Trivy Operator is installed correctly and that the CRDs are applied.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'trivy-system' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may be misconfigured or lacking necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n trivy-system' for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Vulnerability reports are not generated.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Trivy Operator may not be watching the correct namespaces or resources.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the operator has the correct RBAC permissions and is configured to watch the desired namespaces.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to get report: not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified VulnerabilityReport resource may not exist or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the existence of the VulnerabilityReport using 'kubectl get vulnerabilityreport -n <namespace>'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new compliance reports including CIS Kubernetes Benchmark v1.23 and enhanced RBAC scanning capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps required for upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Trivy Operator pods running in the 'trivy-system' namespace and the created VulnerabilityReport should show a status of 'Ready'.

kind: VulnerabilityReport

  namespace: default
spec:
  resource:
    apiVersion: v1
    kind: Pod
    name: sample-pod
    namespace: default
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
